title: Ageria opaca

Ageria opaca is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world, including parts of Asia and Africa. The classification of Ageria opaca is also recognized at the rank of species within the family Rubiaceae. Further research into the specific classification of Ageria opaca may be necessary for more detailed information.

I couldn't find any information on a plant called Ageria opaca. It's possible that this plant does not exist or is not well-documented. However, I can suggest a similar plant, Ageratina pichinchensis, also known as Ageratina opaca, which is a plant species in the family Asteraceae.

Ageratina pichinchensis is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America, characterized by its small white flowers and woody stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to treat various conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Ageratina pichinchensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
